event_claims.actor_label: we and our subsidiaries and our respective representatives
event_claims.description: During the go-shop period, the company, its subsidiaries and their representatives were permitted to solicit other offers directly or indirectly.
event_claims.quote_text: From the date of the merger agreement until 11:59 p.m. New York City time on May 7, 2015, which period we refer to as the "go-shop period," we and our subsidiaries and our respective representatives were permitted to, directly or indirectly:

event_claims.description: The go-shop period ended at 11:59 p.m. New York City time on May 7, 2015.
event_claims.quote_text: From the date of the merger agreement until 11:59 p.m. New York City time on May 7, 2015, which period we refer to as the "go-shop period," we and our subsidiaries and our respective representatives were permitted to, directly or indirectly:

event_claims.actor_label: we and our affiliates and our respective representatives
event_claims.description: After 12:00 a.m. New York City time on May 8, 2015, no-shop restrictions applied to the company, its affiliates and their representatives until the merger effective time or earlier termination.
event_claims.quote_text: Except as otherwise provided in the merger agreement, from and after 12:00 a.m. New York City time on May 8, 2015 until the effective time of the merger, or if earlier, the termination of the merger agreement in accordance with its terms, we and our affiliates and our respective representatives may not:

event_claims.description: Before the required stockholder vote, the company could take actions in response to an unsolicited third-party acquisition proposal that constituted or could reasonably be expected to lead to a superior proposal, subject to specified fiduciary determinations and exceptions for excluded parties.
event_claims.quote_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing restrictions, if, prior to obtaining the required vote of our stockholders to approve and adopt the merger agreement, (i) we receive an unsolicited acquisition proposal from a third party that in the good faith judgment of our board of directors, after consultation with outside legal and financial advisors, constitutes, or would reasonably be expected to lead to, a "superior proposal" (as defined in " *The Merger Agreement—Restrictions on Solicitation of Acquisition Proposals* ") and (ii) other than with respect to taking actions with an excluded party (for so long as such person remains an excluded party), our board of directors determines in good faith, after consultation with outside legal counsel, that the failure to take such action would be reasonably likely to be inconsistent with its fiduciary duties under applicable law, then we may take the following actions:
